title: Enterprise Segmented Architecture Design
shortDescription: Designed a 3-VLAN isolated topology with 802.1Q trunking and strict access control policies.
documentationHtml:
Problem: A business operating on a flat network exposed sensitive core financial and HR data to the guest Wi-Fi broadcast domain, violating security principles.

Solution: Built a Router-on-a-Stick (RoaS) topology separating traffic into VLAN 10 (HR), VLAN 20 (Finance), and VLAN 30 (Guest). Applied extended ACLs to drop guest traffic attempting to cross subnets.

Relationship: While Cisco Packet Tracer simulated the operational logic and visual dropping of unauthorized packets, raw traffic captures from this layout were passed to Wireshark for deep forensic validation.

Key Config:

interface g0/0.30
 encapsulation dot1Q 30
 ip address 192.168.30.1 255.255.255.0
 ip access-group BLOCK_GUEST_IN in
!
ip access-list extended BLOCK_GUEST_IN
 deny ip 192.168.30.0 0.0.0.255 192.168.10.0 0.0.0.255
 permit ip any any

title: Forensic Access Control Policy Verification
shortDescription: Analyzed packet captures to forensically verify broadcast isolation and ACL drop behavior.
documentationHtml:
Symptom: Verification required to guarantee that subnets are completely isolated from broadcast leaks and that malicious traversal attempts are actively blocked by the firewall layer.

Capture Filters: bootp or dhcp (to analyze broadcast limits) and icmp (to verify dropped packets).

Relationship: This analysis serves as the empirical audit for the 'Enterprise Segmented Architecture' project built in Packet Tracer. It captures the exact byte-level evidence that the network configurations perform correctly in production.

Finding: Captured explicit ICMP Type 3, Code 13 (Communication Administratively Prohibited) packets originating from the gateway, proving the Packet Tracer ACL is successfully executing silent drops on unauthorized lateral movement.
